_comment: Guided voice lessons (SESSION_DESIGN.md). Hand-authored; the step kinds map to server lesson.* templates. item_ref/item_refs point at seed vocab so grades land in FSRS; dangling refs are pruned by the importer.

items.title: Meeting a classmate
items.topic_en: introducing yourself to a new classmate on your first day

steps.focus_en: Today we practice self-introductions: greeting someone new, giving your name, and the set phrase that ends every Japanese introduction. First lesson, so keep it warm and simple.

steps.gloss_en: Nice to meet you — the standard opener when meeting someone for the first time

steps.reading: わたしは ジョシュ です
steps.gloss_en: I am Josh — the pattern is 私は NAME です; they should use their own name
accepted: わたしはジョシュです

steps.expectation_en: answer with their name using the 私は〜です pattern
steps.hint_en: Answer with the pattern we just practiced: 私は — your name — です.

steps.target: よろしくお願いします
steps.reading: よろしく おねがいします
steps.gloss_en: Please treat me well — the set phrase that closes an introduction; there is no direct English equivalent
accepted: よろしくお願いします
よろしくおねがいします

steps.focus_en: Now put it together for real: they will meet a friendly classmate and should greet them, give their name, and close with よろしくお願いします. You'll play the classmate.
